title: Working with Charts
text: Charts rpptx embed real Excel workbook inside .pptx file — exactly PowerPoint . workflow : Create CategoryChartData (XyChartData / BubbleChartData) object Add categories series Call slide$shapes$add_chart() XL_CHART_TYPE constant

title: Working with Charts
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  cd <- CategoryChartData$new() cd$categories <- c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4") cd$add_series("Revenue",  c(120, 145, 132, 178)) cd$add_series("Expenses", c(95,  110, 105, 130))  chart_shape <- slide$shapes$add_chart(   XL_CHART_TYPE$COLUMN_CLUSTERED,   left   = Inches(0.5),   top    = Inches(0.5),   width  = Inches(9),   height = Inches(6),   chart_data = cd ) chart_shape$shape_type == MSO_SHAPE_TYPE$CHART #> [1] TRUE

title: Working with Charts
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  cd <- CategoryChartData$new() cd$categories <- c("Product A", "Product B", "Product C") cd$add_series("Units sold", c(450, 320, 580))  slide$shapes$add_chart(   XL_CHART_TYPE$BAR_CLUSTERED,   Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5), Inches(9), Inches(6), cd ) #> <Chart>  "Chart 2"  9.00×6.00 in  @ 0.50, 0.50 in

title: Working with Charts
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  cd <- CategoryChartData$new() cd$categories <- month.abb cd$add_series("2023", c(40, 45, 55, 60, 70, 75, 80, 78, 65, 55, 48, 42)) cd$add_series("2024", c(42, 48, 58, 65, 72, 80, 85, 82, 70, 60, 52, 45))  slide$shapes$add_chart(   XL_CHART_TYPE$LINE,   Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5), Inches(9), Inches(6), cd ) #> <Chart>  "Chart 2"  9.00×6.00 in  @ 0.50, 0.50 in

title: Working with Charts
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  cd <- CategoryChartData$new() cd$categories <- c("North", "South", "East", "West") cd$add_series("Market share", c(35, 25, 20, 20))  slide$shapes$add_chart(   XL_CHART_TYPE$PIE,   Inches(1), Inches(0.5), Inches(8), Inches(6), cd ) #> <Chart>  "Chart 2"  8.00×6.00 in  @ 1.00, 0.50 in

title: Working with Charts
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  cd <- CategoryChartData$new() cd$categories <- paste0("Week ", 1:8) cd$add_series("Team A", c(10, 15, 13, 20, 18, 22, 25, 24)) cd$add_series("Team B", c(8,  12, 10, 16, 14, 18, 20, 19))  slide$shapes$add_chart(   XL_CHART_TYPE$AREA,   Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5), Inches(9), Inches(6), cd ) #> <Chart>  "Chart 2"  9.00×6.00 in  @ 0.50, 0.50 in

title: Working with Charts
text: commonly used XL_CHART_TYPE constants:

title: Working with Charts
text: XY charts need XyChartData accepts (x, y) pairs per series:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  cd <- XyChartData$new() cd$add_series("Series 1",   x_values = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),   y_values = c(2.1, 3.8, 5.2, 7.1, 9.0) ) cd$add_series("Series 2",   x_values = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),   y_values = c(1.5, 2.9, 4.1, 5.8, 7.3) )  slide$shapes$add_chart(   XL_CHART_TYPE$XY_SCATTER,   Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5), Inches(9), Inches(6), cd ) #> <Chart>  "Chart 2"  9.00×6.00 in  @ 0.50, 0.50 in

title: Working with Charts
text: adding chart, retrieve Chart object adjust properties:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  cd <- CategoryChartData$new() cd$categories <- c("A", "B", "C") cd$add_series("S1", c(1, 2, 3))  chart_shape <- slide$shapes$add_chart(   XL_CHART_TYPE$COLUMN_CLUSTERED,   Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5), Inches(9), Inches(6), cd )  chart <- chart_shape$chart class(chart)   # "Chart" #> [1] "Chart" "R6"

title: Working with Charts
code: chart$has_title <- TRUE chart$chart_title$text_frame$text <- "My Chart Title"

title: Working with Charts
text: Legend positions: TOP, BOTTOM, LEFT, RIGHT, CORNER.
code: chart$has_legend <- TRUE chart$legend$position <- XL_LEGEND_POSITION$BOTTOM chart$legend$include_in_layout <- FALSE

title: Working with Charts
code: cat_axis <- chart$category_axis val_axis  <- chart$value_axis  # Axis visibility cat_axis$visible <- TRUE val_axis$visible <- TRUE  # Tick label position cat_axis$tick_label_position <- "low" val_axis$tick_label_position <- "nextTo"  # Scale val_axis$minimum_scale <- 0 val_axis$maximum_scale <- 200

title: Working with Charts
text: Axis tick labels can use Excel number format string:
code: val_axis$tick_labels$number_format <- "0.0%"   # percentage val_axis$tick_labels$number_format <- "#,##0"  # thousands separator val_axis$tick_labels$number_format <- "General" # default

title: Working with Charts
text: Tick mark constants: NONE, INSIDE, OUTSIDE, CROSS.
code: val_axis$major_tick_mark <- XL_TICK_MARK$OUTSIDE val_axis$minor_tick_mark <- XL_TICK_MARK$NONE cat_axis$major_tick_mark <- XL_TICK_MARK$CROSS

title: Working with Charts
code: val_axis$has_major_gridlines <- TRUE val_axis$has_minor_gridlines <- FALSE

title: Working with Charts
code: val_axis$reverse_order <- TRUE   # descending values cat_axis$reverse_order <- FALSE  # ascending categories

title: Working with Charts
code: plot <- chart$plots[[1]] plot$has_data_labels <- TRUE dls <- plot$data_labels dls$show_value         <- TRUE dls$show_category_name <- FALSE dls$show_series_name   <- FALSE

title: Working with Charts
text: series exposes format property fill line sub-objects (shape formatting): Individual data points can also formatted:
code: series <- chart$plots[[1]]$series[[1]] series$format$fill$solid() series$format$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x4F, 0x81, 0xBD) series$format$line$width <- Pt(1.5) series$format$line$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x1F, 0x49, 0x7D) pt <- series$data_points[[2]]   # second point pt$format$fill$solid() pt$format$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0x00, 0x00)

title: Working with Charts
text: chart plot areas format property:
code: chart$format$fill$solid() chart$format$fill$fore_color$theme_color <- MSO_THEME_COLOR$ACCENT_1 chart$format$line$width <- Pt(0.5)

title: Working with Charts
code: # All available chart types length(XL_CHART_TYPE)   # 73+ #> [1] 73 # Common types XL_CHART_TYPE$COLUMN_CLUSTERED XL_CHART_TYPE$BAR_CLUSTERED XL_CHART_TYPE$LINE XL_CHART_TYPE$PIE XL_CHART_TYPE$AREA XL_CHART_TYPE$XY_SCATTER XL_CHART_TYPE$BUBBLE XL_CHART_TYPE$DOUGHNUT XL_CHART_TYPE$RADAR

title: Working with Charts
code: prs    <- pptx_presentation() layout <- prs$slide_layouts[[6]] slide  <- prs$slides$add_slide(layout)  # Build chart data cd <- CategoryChartData$new() cd$categories <- c("Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun") cd$add_series("Actual",   c(120, 135, 128, 155, 162, 175)) cd$add_series("Forecast", c(115, 130, 140, 150, 165, 180))  # Add chart cs <- slide$shapes$add_chart(   XL_CHART_TYPE$LINE_MARKERS,   Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5),   Inches(9),   Inches(6.5),   cd )  # Decorate ch <- cs$chart ch$has_title <- TRUE ch$chart_title$text_frame$text <- "H1 Performance" ch$has_legend <- TRUE ch$legend$position <- XL_LEGEND_POSITION$BOTTOM  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".pptx") prs$save(tmp)

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
text: RGBColor holds red–green–blue triple (0–255 ):
code: red   <- RGBColor(255L, 0L, 0L) green <- RGBColor(0L, 128L, 0L) blue  <- RGBColor_from_str("0000FF")   # from hex string  print(red)     # RGBColor(255, 0, 0) [#FF0000] #> RGBColor(255, 0, 0) [#FF0000] as.character(blue)  # "0000FF" #> [1] "0000FF" red == RGBColor(255L, 0L, 0L)  # TRUE #> [1] TRUE

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
text: Call fill$solid() set foreground color:
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide)  shape$fill$solid() shape$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x4F, 0x81, 0xBD) shape$fill$type   # "solid" #> [1] "solid"

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
text: Use MSO_THEME_COLOR value apply color follows presentation theme: Apply tint (lighten toward white) shade (darken toward black):
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide)  shape$fill$solid() shape$fill$fore_color$theme_color <- MSO_THEME_COLOR$ACCENT_1 shape$fill$fore_color$type   # "scheme" #> [1] "scheme" r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide)  shape$fill$solid() shape$fill$fore_color$theme_color <- MSO_THEME_COLOR$ACCENT_1 shape$fill$fore_color$tint  <- 0.4   # 40 % lighter shape$fill$fore_color$shade <- NULL  # remove shade (if any)

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide) shape$fill$background() shape$fill$type   # "background" #> NULL

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
text: fill$gradient() creates default two-stop linear gradient. Customise stops angle afterwards:
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide)  shape$fill$gradient() shape$fill$gradient_angle <- 270.0   # top-to-bottom  stops <- shape$fill$gradient_stops stops[[1]]$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x4F, 0x81, 0xBD)  # start color stops[[2]]$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xBD, 0xD7, 0xEE)  # end color  length(stops)               # 2 #> [1] 2 stops[[1]]$position         # 0 #> [1] 0 stops[[2]]$position         # 1 #> [1] 1

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
text: fill$patterned() creates hatched pattern. Set preset fill$pattern control foreground/background colors fore_color/back_color:
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide)  shape$fill$patterned() shape$fill$pattern <- MSO_PATTERN_TYPE$DIAGONAL_BRICK shape$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x4F, 0x81, 0xBD) shape$fill$back_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF)  shape$fill$type     # "patterned" #> [1] "patterned" shape$fill$pattern  # "diagBrick" #> [1] "diagBrick"

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
text: Access line properties via shape$line: Set NULL remove width override:
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide)  shape$line$width     <- Pt(2)                         # 2-point line shape$line$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0, 0)          # red shape$line$dash_style <- MSO_LINE_DASH_STYLE$DASH      # dashed  shape$line$width      # 182880 EMU ≈ 2 pt #> <Length: 25400 EMU (0.03 in, 0.07 cm, 2.0 pt)> shape$line$width <- NULL   # inherit from theme

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
text: shape$shadow exposes ShadowFormat object:
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide shape <- new_rect(slide) shape$fill$solid()  shadow <- shape$shadow shadow$inherit        # TRUE (inherits from theme) #> [1] TRUE shadow$visible        # FALSE by default unless theme enables it #> NULL

title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
text: slide exposes background object fill property. Accessing background$fill interrupts master-level background inheritance applies explicit fill slide:
code: r <- blank_slide(); slide <- r$slide slide$background$fill$solid() slide$background$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F)

what: Putting it together: a styled shape
title: Colors, Fills, and Lines
code: r <- blank_slide() prs   <- r$prs slide <- r$slide  shape <- slide$shapes$add_shape(   MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$ROUNDED_RECTANGLE,   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(4), Inches(2) )  # Gradient fill: blue to sky-blue shape$fill$gradient() shape$fill$gradient_angle <- 135.0 shape$fill$gradient_stops[[1]]$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x00, 0x70, 0xC0) shape$fill$gradient_stops[[2]]$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x9D, 0xC3, 0xE6)  # White border shape$line$width     <- Pt(1.5) shape$line$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF)  # Text tf  <- shape$text_frame run <- tf$paragraphs[[1]]$add_run() run$text <- "Styled shape" tf$paragraphs[[1]]$runs[[1]]$font$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF) tf$paragraphs[[1]]$runs[[1]]$font$bold      <- TRUE  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".pptx") prs$save(tmp)

title: Getting Started with rpptx
text: pptx_presentation() opens existing file — argument — creates new blank presentation based built-default template. Check slide dimensions (EMU — English Metric Units, 914400 per inch): Use Inches(), Cm(), Pt() convert convenient units EMU: convert back EMU readable unit use as_inches(), as_cm(), as_mm(), as_pt():
code: prs <- pptx_presentation() prs$slide_width   # 10 inches #> <Length: 9144000 EMU (10.00 in, 25.40 cm, 720.0 pt)> prs$slide_height  # 7.5 inches #> <Length: 6858000 EMU (7.50 in, 19.05 cm, 540.0 pt)> Inches(1)     # 914400L #> <Length: 914400 EMU (1.00 in, 2.54 cm, 72.0 pt)> Cm(2.54)      # 914400L #> <Length: 914400 EMU (1.00 in, 2.54 cm, 72.0 pt)> Pt(72)        # 914400L #> <Length: 914400 EMU (1.00 in, 2.54 cm, 72.0 pt)> as_inches(prs$slide_width)   # 10 #> [1] 10 as_cm(prs$slide_width)       # 25.4 #> [1] 25.4 as_pt(Pt(18))                # 18 #> [1] 18

title: Getting Started with rpptx
text: Slides added Slides collection using slide layout template. Every new blank presentation one slide master eleven layouts. Add blank slide (layout index 6 “Blank” default template): Access individual slides 1-based index:
code: # List available layout names sapply(seq_len(length(prs$slide_layouts)), function(i) {   prs$slide_layouts[[i]]$name }) #>  [1] "Title Slide"             "Title and Content"       #>  [3] "Section Header"          "Two Content"             #>  [5] "Comparison"              "Title Only"              #>  [7] "Blank"                   "Content with Caption"    #>  [9] "Picture with Caption"    "Title and Vertical Text" #> [11] "Vertical Title and Text" layout <- prs$slide_layouts[[6]] slide  <- prs$slides$add_slide(layout) length(prs$slides)    # 1 #> [1] 1 prs$slides[[1]]       # the slide we just added #> <Slide>  1 shape #>   Placeholder     Title                     9.00×1.25 in @ 0.50, 0.30

title: Getting Started with rpptx
code: slide$shapes$add_textbox(   left   = Inches(1),   top    = Inches(1),   width  = Inches(5),   height = Inches(1) ) #> <TextBox>  "TextBox 2"  5.00×1.00 in  @ 1.00, 1.00 in shape <- slide$shapes[[1]] shape$text_frame$text <- "Hello, rpptx!"

title: Getting Started with rpptx
code: rect <- slide$shapes$add_shape(   MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$ROUNDED_RECTANGLE,   left   = Inches(1),   top    = Inches(2.5),   width  = Inches(3),   height = Inches(1.5) ) rect$text <- "A rounded rectangle" rect$fill$solid() rect$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x46L, 0x72L, 0xC4L)

title: Getting Started with rpptx
code: slide$shapes$add_picture(   "path/to/photo.jpg",   left   = Inches(5),   top    = Inches(2),   width  = Inches(3),   height = Inches(2) )

title: Getting Started with rpptx
text: save temp file:
code: prs$save("my_presentation.pptx") tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".pptx") prs$save(tmp) file.exists(tmp) #> [1] TRUE

title: Getting Started with rpptx
code: prs2 <- pptx_presentation("existing.pptx") slide1 <- prs2$slides[[1]] for (shape in slide1$shapes$to_list()) {   cat(shape$name, "\n") }

title: Getting Started with rpptx
code: prs2 <- pptx_presentation() layout <- prs2$slide_layouts[[6]] s1 <- prs2$slides$add_slide(layout) s2 <- prs2$slides$add_slide(layout) length(prs2$slides)   # 2 #> [1] 2 prs2$slides$delete(s1) length(prs2$slides)   # 1 #> [1] 1

title: Getting Started with rpptx
code: prs3 <- pptx_presentation() layout <- prs3$slide_layouts[[6]] for (i in 1:3) prs3$slides$add_slide(layout) id_last <- prs3$slides[[3]]$slide_id prs3$slides$move(prs3$slides[[3]], 1L) prs3$slides[[1]]$slide_id == id_last  # TRUE #> [1] TRUE

title: Getting Started with rpptx
code: prs4 <- pptx_presentation() cp <- prs4$core_properties cp$title   <- "My Presentation" cp$author  <- "Jane Doe" cp$subject <- "rpptx demo" cp$title #> [1] "My Presentation"

title: Working with Shapes
text: Every shape slide shape_type identifies :

title: Working with Shapes
text: shapes share properties: Positions sizes read/write:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide shape <- slide$shapes$add_shape(   MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RECTANGLE,   left = Inches(1), top = Inches(1),   width = Inches(3), height = Inches(2) )  shape$name         # "Rectangle 1" #> [1] "AutoShape 2" shape$shape_id     # integer ID unique within the slide #> [1] 3 shape$left         # position in EMU #> <Length: 914400 EMU (1.00 in, 2.54 cm, 72.0 pt)> shape$top #> <Length: 914400 EMU (1.00 in, 2.54 cm, 72.0 pt)> shape$width #> <Length: 2743200 EMU (3.00 in, 7.62 cm, 216.0 pt)> shape$height #> <Length: 1828800 EMU (2.00 in, 5.08 cm, 144.0 pt)> shape$shape_type   # MSO_SHAPE_TYPE$AUTO_SHAPE #> [1] 1 shape$left   <- Inches(2) shape$top    <- Inches(3) shape$width  <- Inches(4) shape$height <- Inches(1.5)

title: Working with Shapes
text: add_shape() accepts MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE constant: commonly used shape types:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  rect  <- slide$shapes$add_shape(MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RECTANGLE,            Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5), Inches(2), Inches(1.5)) oval  <- slide$shapes$add_shape(MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$OVAL,            Inches(3),   Inches(0.5), Inches(2), Inches(1.5)) arrow <- slide$shapes$add_shape(MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RIGHT_ARROW,            Inches(5.5), Inches(0.5), Inches(3), Inches(1.5)) MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RECTANGLE #> $value #> [1] 1 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "rect" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$ROUNDED_RECTANGLE #> $value #> [1] 5 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "roundRect" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$OVAL #> $value #> [1] 9 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "ellipse" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$DIAMOND #> $value #> [1] 4 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "diamond" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$TRIANGLE #> NULL MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RIGHT_ARROW #> $value #> [1] 33 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "rightArrow" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$STAR_5_POINT #> $value #> [1] 92 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "star5" MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$CLOUD #> $value #> [1] 179 #>  #> $prst #> [1] "cloud"

title: Working with Shapes
text: text box shape preset geometry:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  tb <- slide$shapes$add_textbox(   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(5), Inches(1) ) tb$text_frame$text <- "Hello from a text box" tb$shape_type == MSO_SHAPE_TYPE$TEXT_BOX #> [1] TRUE

title: Working with Shapes
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  pic <- slide$shapes$add_picture(   "photo.jpg",   left = Inches(1), top = Inches(1),   width = Inches(4), height = Inches(3) ) pic$shape_type == MSO_SHAPE_TYPE$PICTURE

title: Working with Shapes
text: Connectors link two points line: Connector types: STRAIGHT, ELBOW, CURVE.
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  conn <- slide$shapes$add_connector(   MSO_CONNECTOR_TYPE$STRAIGHT,   begin_x = Inches(1), begin_y = Inches(1),   end_x   = Inches(5), end_y   = Inches(4) ) conn$shape_type == MSO_SHAPE_TYPE$LINE #> [1] TRUE

title: Working with Shapes
text: Shapes (autoshapes, text boxes) expose fill property: Fill types:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide shape <- slide$shapes$add_shape(   MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RECTANGLE,   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(3), Inches(2) )  # Solid fill with RGB colour shape$fill$solid() shape$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x4F, 0x81, 0xBD)  # No fill (transparent) shape$fill$background()

title: Working with Shapes
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide shape <- slide$shapes$add_shape(   MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RECTANGLE,   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(3), Inches(2) )  shape$line$width                  <- Pt(2) shape$line$color$rgb              <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0x00, 0x00) shape$line$dash_style             <- MSO_LINE_DASH_STYLE$DASH

title: Working with Shapes
text: SlideShapes supports 1-based index access to_list():
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide slide$shapes$add_shape(MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RECTANGLE,   Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5), Inches(2), Inches(1)) #> <AutoShape>  "AutoShape 2"  2.00×1.00 in  @ 0.50, 0.50 in slide$shapes$add_textbox(   Inches(3), Inches(0.5), Inches(3), Inches(1)) #> <TextBox>  "TextBox 3"  3.00×1.00 in  @ 3.00, 0.50 in  length(slide$shapes)   # 2 #> [1] 3 slide$shapes[[1]]$name #> [1] "Title"  for (shape in slide$shapes$to_list()) {   cat(shape$name, "—", shape$shape_type, "\n") } #> Title — 14  #> AutoShape 2 — 1  #> TextBox 3 — 17

title: Working with Shapes
text: Multiple shapes can grouped:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide s1 <- slide$shapes$add_shape(MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$RECTANGLE,         Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(2), Inches(1)) s2 <- slide$shapes$add_shape(MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$OVAL,         Inches(2), Inches(2), Inches(2), Inches(1))  grp <- slide$shapes$add_group_shape(list(s1, s2)) grp$shape_type == MSO_SHAPE_TYPE$GROUP #> [1] TRUE length(grp$shapes)   # 2 #> [1] 2

title: Working with Shapes
text: Placeholder shapes (slide layout) accessed via placeholders: Set placeholder text: ([[n]] SlidePlaceholders accepts 1-based position, OOXML idx.)
code: prs    <- pptx_presentation() layout <- prs$slide_layouts[[1]]   # "Title Slide" layout slide  <- prs$slides$add_slide(layout)  # List placeholder indices and types for (ph in slide$placeholders$to_list()) {   cat(ph$placeholder_format$idx, "—", ph$placeholder_format$type, "\n") } #> 0 — ctrTitle  #> 1 — subTitle ph1 <- slide$placeholders[[1]]; ph1$text <- "My Title" ph2 <- slide$placeholders[[2]]; ph2$text <- "My Subtitle"

title: Working with Shapes
text: Embed video file slide add_movie(). shape shows poster frame image video plays. default poster frame generic loudspeaker icon bundled package. Supply image poster_frame_image: video relationship uses OOXML dual-relationship pattern required Office 2007/2010 compatibility: one RT.MEDIA relationship one RT.VIDEO relationship pointing media part. Multiple calls file reuse single media part (SHA-1 deduplication).
code: mp4 <- system.file("test_files", "dummy.mp4", package = "rpptx")  r     <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  shape <- slide$shapes$add_movie(   movie_file = mp4,   left = Inches(1), top = Inches(1),   width = Inches(6), height = Inches(4) ) shape$shape_type  # MSO_SHAPE_TYPE$PICTURE img <- system.file("test_files", "monty-truth.png", package = "rpptx") slide$shapes$add_movie(mp4,   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(6), Inches(4),   poster_frame_image = img,   mime_type = "video/mp4" )

title: Working with Tables
text: add_table() adds empty table given number rows columns:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  tbl_shape <- slide$shapes$add_table(   rows   = 4,   cols   = 3,   left   = Inches(1),   top    = Inches(1),   width  = Inches(8),   height = Inches(3) ) tbl <- tbl_shape$table class(tbl)   # "Table" #> [1] "Table" "R6"

title: Working with Tables
code: tbl$rows     # 4 #> <TableRows> #>   Public: #>     clone: function (deep = FALSE)  #>     get: function (idx)  #>     initialize: function (tbl, parent)  #>     to_list: function ()  #>   Private: #>     .parent: Table, R6 #>     .tbl: CT_Table, BaseOxmlElement, R6 tbl$columns  # 3 #> <TableColumns> #>   Public: #>     clone: function (deep = FALSE)  #>     get: function (idx)  #>     initialize: function (tbl, parent)  #>     to_list: function ()  #>   Private: #>     .parent: Table, R6 #>     .tbl: CT_Table, BaseOxmlElement, R6

title: Working with Tables
text: cell(row, col) uses 1-based indices:
code: cell <- tbl$cell(1, 1) class(cell)   # "_Cell" #> [1] "TableCell" "R6"

title: Working with Tables
code: cell_11 <- tbl$cell(1, 1); cell_11$text <- "Header A" cell_12 <- tbl$cell(1, 2); cell_12$text <- "Header B" cell_13 <- tbl$cell(1, 3); cell_13$text <- "Header C"  cell_21 <- tbl$cell(2, 1); cell_21$text <- "Row 1" cell_22 <- tbl$cell(2, 2); cell_22$text <- "42" cell_23 <- tbl$cell(2, 3); cell_23$text <- "3.14"

what: Filling a table from a data frame
title: Working with Tables
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide  df <- data.frame(   Name    = c("Alice", "Bob", "Carol"),   Score   = c(92, 85, 78),   Grade   = c("A", "B+", "B"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  nr   <- nrow(df) + 1L   # +1 for header nc   <- ncol(df)  tbl_shape <- slide$shapes$add_table(   nr, nc,   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(8), Inches(3.5) ) tbl <- tbl_shape$table  # Write column headers for (j in seq_len(nc)) {   cell <- tbl$cell(1, j)   cell$text <- names(df)[j] }  # Write data rows for (i in seq_len(nrow(df))) {   for (j in seq_len(nc)) {     cell <- tbl$cell(i + 1L, j)     cell$text <- as.character(df[i, j])   } }

title: Working with Tables
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide tbl_shape <- slide$shapes$add_table(3, 3, Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(7), Inches(3)) tbl <- tbl_shape$table  # Blue header row for (j in 1:3) {   cell <- tbl$cell(1, j)   cell$fill$solid()   cell$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x4F, 0x81, 0xBD) }  # Alternating row shading for (i in 2:3) {   for (j in 1:3) {     cell <- tbl$cell(i, j)     if (i %% 2 == 0) {       cell$fill$solid()       cell$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xDB, 0xE5, 0xF1)     } else {       cell$fill$background()     }   } }

title: Working with Tables
text: cell text_frame rich text formatting:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide tbl_shape <- slide$shapes$add_table(2, 2, Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(6), Inches(2)) tbl <- tbl_shape$table  # Bold header cell <- tbl$cell(1, 1) cell$text <- "Total" cell$text_frame$paragraphs[[1]]$font$bold <- TRUE  # Right-aligned number cell2 <- tbl$cell(2, 1) cell2$text <- "1,234" cell2$text_frame$paragraphs[[1]]$alignment <- PP_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT$RIGHT

title: Working with Tables
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide tbl_shape <- slide$shapes$add_table(3, 3, Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(8), Inches(3)) tbl <- tbl_shape$table  # Set column widths tbl$columns[[1]]$width <- Inches(3) tbl$columns[[2]]$width <- Inches(2) tbl$columns[[3]]$width <- Inches(3)  # Set row heights tbl$rows[[1]]$height <- Inches(0.6) tbl$rows[[2]]$height <- Inches(1.0) tbl$rows[[3]]$height <- Inches(1.0)

title: Working with Tables
text: merge() spans rectangular block cells:
code: r <- blank_slide() slide <- r$slide tbl_shape <- slide$shapes$add_table(3, 4, Inches(0.5), Inches(1), Inches(9), Inches(3)) tbl <- tbl_shape$table  # Merge the top row across all 4 columns cell_11 <- tbl$cell(1, 1) cell_14 <- tbl$cell(1, 4) cell_11$merge(cell_14) cell_11$text <- "Merged Header"

title: Working with Tables
code: prs    <- pptx_presentation() layout <- prs$slide_layouts[[6]] slide  <- prs$slides$add_slide(layout)  # Sample data sales <- data.frame(   Region  = c("North", "South", "East", "West", "Total"),   Q1      = c(234, 187, 312, 145, 878),   Q2      = c(256, 201, 298, 167, 922),   Q3      = c(289, 223, 341, 189, 1042),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  nr <- nrow(sales) + 1L nc <- ncol(sales)  ts <- slide$shapes$add_table(   nr, nc,   Inches(0.5), Inches(0.5),   Inches(9),   Inches(5) ) tbl <- ts$table  # Headers header_color <- RGBColor(0x17, 0x37, 0x5E) for (j in seq_len(nc)) {   cell <- tbl$cell(1, j)   cell$text <- names(sales)[j]   cell$fill$solid()   cell$fill$fore_color$rgb <- header_color   tf <- cell$text_frame   tf$paragraphs[[1]]$font$bold  <- TRUE   tf$paragraphs[[1]]$font$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF) }  # Data rows for (i in seq_len(nrow(sales))) {   row_color <- if (i %% 2 == 1) RGBColor(0xD6, 0xDC, 0xE4) else RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF)   is_total  <- sales$Region[i] == "Total"   for (j in seq_len(nc)) {     cell <- tbl$cell(i + 1L, j)     cell$text <- as.character(sales[i, j])     cell$fill$solid()     cell$fill$fore_color$rgb <- if (is_total) header_color else row_color     if (is_total) {       cell$text_frame$paragraphs[[1]]$font$bold  <- TRUE       cell$text_frame$paragraphs[[1]]$font$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF)     }     if (j > 1) {       cell$text_frame$paragraphs[[1]]$alignment <- PP_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT$RIGHT     }   } }  # Column widths tbl$columns[[1]]$width <- Inches(2) for (j in 2:nc) tbl$columns[[j]]$width <- Inches(7 / (nc - 1))  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".pptx") prs$save(tmp)

title: Working with Text
text: Text rpptx follows three-level hierarchy mirroring OOXML structure: TextFrame belongs shape contains one Paragraph objects. Paragraph contains one Run objects. Runs smallest unit independent character formatting.
code: TextFrame  (p:txBody)   └─ Paragraph  (a:p)        └─ Run  (a:r)

title: Working with Text
text: Shape (autoshape text box) has_text_frame == TRUE exposes text_frame:
code: shape <- shape_with_text() tf    <- shape$text_frame class(tf)  # "TextFrame" #> [1] "TextFrame" "R6"

title: Working with Text
text: convenience, shape$text reads/writes text single string: text_frame$text frame level:
code: shape <- shape_with_text() shape$text <- "Hello world" shape$text #> [1] "Hello world" shape$text_frame$text <- "Updated text" shape$text_frame$text #> [1] "Updated text"

title: Working with Text
text: Paragraph alignment: Alignment values: LEFT, CENTER, RIGHT, JUSTIFY, DISTRIBUTE. Indentation level (0–8): Line paragraph spacing (points):
code: shape <- shape_with_text() tf    <- shape$text_frame  # Add paragraphs p1 <- tf$add_paragraph() p1$text <- "First paragraph"  p2 <- tf$add_paragraph() p2$text <- "Second paragraph"  length(tf$paragraphs)  # 2 (there's always a default empty paragraph first) #> [1] 3 p1$alignment <- PP_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT$CENTER p2$alignment <- PP_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT$RIGHT p2$level <- 1L   # indent one level p1$line_spacing  <- 1.5    # 1.5× line height p1$space_before  <- Pt(6) p1$space_after   <- Pt(12)

title: Working with Text
text: run carries font formatting. Add runs paragraph:
code: shape <- shape_with_text() tf    <- shape$text_frame tf$clear()  p <- tf$add_paragraph() r1 <- p$add_run(); r1$text <- "Bold " r2 <- p$add_run(); r2$text <- "and italic"

title: Working with Text
text: Access paragraph-level default font (applies runs without explicit overrides):
code: r1$font$bold   <- TRUE r2$font$italic <- TRUE r1$font$size          <- Pt(18)        # font size r1$font$name          <- "Calibri" r1$font$color$rgb     <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0x00, 0x00)  # red r1$font$underline     <- TRUE p$font$bold <- TRUE    # makes all runs in p bold by default

title: Working with Text
code: r2$font$color$theme_color <- MSO_THEME_COLOR$ACCENT_1

title: Working with Text
code: r1$font$bold  <- NULL   # removes override, inherits from theme r1$font$size  <- NULL

title: Working with Text
text: Attach URL run: Read back:
code: shape <- shape_with_text() tf    <- shape$text_frame tf$clear() p <- tf$add_paragraph() r <- p$add_run() r$text <- "Visit our website" r$hyperlink$address <- "https://example.com" r$hyperlink$address   # "https://example.com"

title: Working with Text
code: shape <- shape_with_text() shape$text_frame$word_wrap <- TRUE   # wrap text (default) shape$text_frame$word_wrap <- FALSE  # no wrap

title: Working with Text
text: Values: TOP, MIDDLE, BOTTOM.
code: shape$text_frame$vertical_anchor <- MSO_VERTICAL_ANCHOR$MIDDLE

title: Working with Text
code: tf <- shape$text_frame tf$margin_left   <- Inches(0.1) tf$margin_right  <- Inches(0.1) tf$margin_top    <- Inches(0.05) tf$margin_bottom <- Inches(0.05)

title: Working with Text
code: prs    <- pptx_presentation() layout <- prs$slide_layouts[[6]] slide  <- prs$slides$add_slide(layout)  tb <- slide$shapes$add_textbox(   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(8), Inches(5.5) ) tf <- tb$text_frame tf$word_wrap <- TRUE  # First paragraph — heading style tf$clear() p1 <- tf$paragraphs[[1]] p1$text <- "rpptx Text Demo" p1$font$bold <- TRUE p1$font$size <- Pt(28) p1$alignment <- PP_PARAGRAPH_ALIGNMENT$CENTER  # Second paragraph — normal text with mixed formatting p2 <- tf$add_paragraph() p2$space_before <- Pt(12) r1 <- p2$add_run(); r1$text <- "You can mix " r2 <- p2$add_run(); r2$text <- "bold"; r2$font$bold <- TRUE r3 <- p2$add_run(); r3$text <- ", " r4 <- p2$add_run(); r4$text <- "italic"; r4$font$italic <- TRUE r5 <- p2$add_run(); r5$text <- ", and " r6 <- p2$add_run() r6$text <- "coloured" r6$font$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xC0, 0x00, 0x00) r7 <- p2$add_run(); r7$text <- " text freely."  # Third paragraph — bulleted list simulation bullets <- c("First item", "Second item", "Third item") for (b in bullets) {   p <- tf$add_paragraph()   p$text  <- b   p$level <- 1L   p$space_before <- Pt(4) }  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".pptx") prs$save(tmp)

title: Working with Text
text: slide can speaker notes accessible via notes_text_frame:
code: prs    <- pptx_presentation() layout <- prs$slide_layouts[[6]] slide  <- prs$slides$add_slide(layout)  ns  <- slide$notes_slide ntf <- ns$notes_text_frame ntf$text <- "These are the speaker notes for this slide." ntf$text #> [1] "These are the speaker notes for this slide."

title: Authors and Citation
text: Mark Heckmann. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Heckmann M (2026). rpptx: Create, Read, Update PowerPoint Presentations. R package version 0.1.0, https://markheckmann.github.io/rpptx.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rpptx: Create, Read, and Update PowerPoint Presentations},   author = {Mark Heckmann},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://markheckmann.github.io/rpptx}, }

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
text: rpptx R package creating, reading, updating PowerPoint (.pptx) presentations. close R port Python python-pptx library, providing R6-based object model mirrors Python API.

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: # Development version from GitHub: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("markheckmann/rpptx")

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: library(rpptx)  # ---- Open / create ----------------------------------------------------------- prs <- pptx_presentation()                    # blank presentation prs <- pptx_presentation("my_file.pptx")      # open existing  # ---- Add a slide ------------------------------------------------------------- layout <- prs$slide_layouts[[1]]              # "Title Slide" layout slide  <- prs$slides$add_slide(layout)  # ---- Add shapes -------------------------------------------------------------- # Text box box <- slide$shapes$add_textbox(Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(4), Inches(1)) tf  <- box$text_frame tf$text <- "Hello, rpptx!"  # Autoshape (filled rectangle) shp <- slide$shapes$add_shape(   MSO_AUTO_SHAPE_TYPE$ROUNDED_RECTANGLE,   Inches(1), Inches(2.5), Inches(3), Inches(1.5) ) shp$fill$solid() shp$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x4F, 0x81, 0xBD)  # ---- Save -------------------------------------------------------------------- prs$save("output.pptx")

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: prs$slide_width   # Emu (use .emu_in() or as.numeric() / 914400) prs$slide_height prs$slides        # collection: prs$slides[[1]], length(prs$slides) prs$slide_layouts # 11 default layouts prs$slide_masters prs$core_properties$title <- "My Presentation"

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: tf <- shape$text_frame tf$text <- "Replace all text" tf$word_wrap <- TRUE tf$auto_size <- MSO_AUTO_SIZE$SHAPE_TO_FIT_TEXT   # expand shape to fit text tf$vertical_anchor <- MSO_VERTICAL_ANCHOR$MIDDLE  para <- tf$paragraphs[[1]] para$alignment <- PP_ALIGN$CENTER run  <- para$add_run() run$text <- "Bold blue text" run$font$bold  <- TRUE run$font$size  <- Pt(24) run$font$color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0x00, 0x00)

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: gf  <- slide$shapes$add_table(3, 4, Inches(1), Inches(2), Inches(8), Inches(3)) tbl <- gf$table  # Set cell text cell <- tbl$cell(1, 1) cell$text <- "Header"  # Style a cell cell$fill$solid() cell$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0x4F, 0x81, 0xBD)  # Convert to data frame df <- as.data.frame(tbl)

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: # Column chart chart_data <- CategoryChartData$new() chart_data$categories <- c("East", "West", "North") chart_data$add_series("Q1", c(19.2, 21.4, 16.7)) chart_data$add_series("Q2", c(22.3, 28.6, 15.2))  gf <- slide$shapes$add_chart(   XL_CHART_TYPE$COLUMN_CLUSTERED,   Inches(1), Inches(1), Inches(8), Inches(5),   chart_data )  # Scatter (XY) chart xy_data <- XyChartData$new() s <- xy_data$add_series("Series 1") s$add_data_point(1.0, 2.3) s$add_data_point(1.5, 1.8) slide$shapes$add_chart(XL_CHART_TYPE$XY_SCATTER, ...)  # Bubble chart bd <- BubbleChartData$new() s  <- bd$add_series("Bubbles") s$add_data_point(x = 1.0, y = 2.0, size = 10) s$add_data_point(x = 2.0, y = 3.0, size = 20) slide$shapes$add_chart(XL_CHART_TYPE$BUBBLE, ...)

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: pic <- slide$shapes$add_picture(   "photo.jpg",   left  = Inches(1),   top   = Inches(1),   width = Inches(4)     # height auto-calculated if omitted )

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: # Local coordinate system where 100 units = 1 inch scale <- Inches(1) / 100  ff  <- slide$shapes$build_freeform(start_x = 0, start_y = 0, scale = scale) ff$add_line_segments(list(c(100, 0), c(50, 87), c(0, 0)))   # triangle shp <- ff$convert_to_shape(origin_x = Inches(1), origin_y = Inches(1))

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: # Solid fill shp$fill$solid() shp$fill$fore_color$rgb <- RGBColor(0xFF, 0x00, 0x00)  # Theme color shp$fill$fore_color$theme_color <- MSO_THEME_COLOR$ACCENT_1  # Line shp$line$width <- Pt(2) shp$line$dash_style <- MSO_LINE_DASH_STYLE$DASH  # No fill shp$fill$background()

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: conn <- slide$shapes$add_connector(   MSO_CONNECTOR_TYPE$STRAIGHT,   begin_x = Inches(1), begin_y = Inches(1),   end_x   = Inches(3), end_y   = Inches(3) )

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: # Group two existing shapes grp <- slide$shapes$add_group_shape(list(shape1, shape2))

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: prs               # <Presentation>  slides: 3  size: 10.00 × 7.50 in ... slide             # <Slide>  9 shapes  ... shape             # <AutoShape>  "Rectangle 1"  3.00×2.00 in @ 1.00, 1.00 in tbl               # <Table>  3 rows × 4 cols

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
code: Inches(1.5)       # 1371600 EMU Cm(2.54)          # 914400 EMU (= 1 inch) Pt(18)            # 228600 EMU Mm(25.4)          # 914400 EMU Emu(914400)       # explicit EMU

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
text: rpptx direct R port python-pptx. object model, property names, API intentionally kept close possible Python original. Key differences:

title: rpptx — PowerPoint from R
text: MIT

title: Freeform shape builder — FreeformBuilder
text: Specifies creates freeform (custom-geometry) shape. Obtain via slide$shapes$build_freeform().

title: Freeform shape builder — FreeformBuilder
text: add_line_segments(vertices, close = TRUE) Add line segments current position point     vertices (list c(x, y) vectors local     coordinates). close TRUE (default) closing     segment appended. Returns self invisibly chaining. move_to(x, y) Move pen (x, y) without drawing. Returns self     invisibly chaining. convert_to_shape(origin_x = Emu(0), origin_y = Emu(0)) Create freeform shape slide. origin_x     origin_y (EMU) locate local-coordinate origin     slide. Returns new Shape proxy object. shape_offset_x() Return x coordinate leftmost extent (local units). shape_offset_y() Return y coordinate topmost extent (local units).

title: Freeform shape builder — FreeformBuilder
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ prs   <- pptx_presentation() slide <- prs$slides$add_slide(prs$slide_layouts[[6]])  # local coords where 100 units = 1 inch scale <- Inches(1) / 100  ff <- slide$shapes$build_freeform(start_x = 0, start_y = 0, scale = scale) ff$add_line_segments(list(c(100, 0), c(50, 87), c(0, 0)))  # triangle shp <- ff$convert_to_shape(origin_x = Inches(1), origin_y = Inches(1)) prs$save("output.pptx") } # }

what: Create a Length value in English Metric Units (EMU) — Length
title: Create a Length value in English Metric Units (EMU) — Length
text: Base constructor length values. length values stored integer EMU values. Use convenience constructors Inches(), Cm(), Mm(), Pt(), Emu(), Centipoints() common units.

title: Create a Length value in English Metric Units (EMU) — Length
code: Length(emu)  Inches(inches)  Cm(cm)  Mm(mm)  Pt(pt)  Emu(emu)  Centipoints(centipoints)

title: Create a Length value in English Metric Units (EMU) — Length
text: emu Integer EMU value. inches Numeric length inches. cm Numeric length centimeters. mm Numeric length millimeters. pt Numeric length points. centipoints Integer length hundredths point (1/7200 inch).

what: rpptx: Create, Read, and Update PowerPoint Presentations — rpptx-package
title: rpptx: Create, Read, and Update PowerPoint Presentations — rpptx-package
text: Create, read, update PowerPoint (.pptx) files. R port Python 'python-pptx' library. Provides R6-based object model manipulating presentations, slides, shapes, text, charts, tables, formatting properties. Supports autoshapes, text boxes, pictures, tables, charts (column, bar, line, pie, scatter, bubble, radar, doughnut), fills, lines, colors, freeform custom-geometry shapes, placeholder inheritance.

title: rpptx: Create, Read, and Update PowerPoint Presentations — rpptx-package
text: Maintainer: Mark Heckmann heckmann.mark@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: rpptx 0.1.0
text: Full read/write support PowerPoint (.pptx) files.

title: rpptx 0.1.0
text: pptx_presentation() — open create presentation Presentation$save() — save disk Presentation$slide_width, $slide_height — slide dimensions Presentation$slides, $slide_layouts, $slide_masters — collections Presentation$core_properties — document metadata

title: rpptx 0.1.0
text: Slides$add_slide() — add new slide layout Slides$delete() — remove slide presentation Slides$move() — reorder slides moving slide target position Slide$shapes — shape collection slide Slide$placeholders — placeholder collection Slide$notes_slide — access (lazily create) notes slide slide Slide$has_notes_slide — TRUE notes slide exists slide SlideLayout$placeholders, SlideMaster$placeholders — layout/master placeholders Placeholder inheritance: slides inherit position/size layout master

title: rpptx 0.1.0
text: shape types supported: Shape, Picture, GraphicFrame, GroupShape, Connector, SlidePlaceholder, LayoutPlaceholder, MasterPlaceholder Position size: $left, $top, $width, $height $name, $shape_id, $shape_type, $has_text_frame

title: rpptx 0.1.0
text: slide$shapes$add_shape() — autoshape slide$shapes$add_textbox() — text box slide$shapes$add_picture() — image (PNG, JPEG, etc. via magick) slide$shapes$add_table() — table slide$shapes$add_chart() — chart slide$shapes$add_connector() — connector slide$shapes$add_group_shape() — group existing shapes slide$shapes$build_freeform() — custom-geometry freeform shapes slide$shapes$add_movie() — embed video file (OOXML dual-relationship pattern)

title: rpptx 0.1.0
text: shape$text_frame — TextFrame $paragraphs, $text, $word_wrap, $vertical_anchor, margins paragraph$runs, paragraph$add_run(), paragraph$alignment run$text, run$font — Font $bold, $italic, $underline, $size, $name, $color

title: rpptx 0.1.0
text: shape$table — Table $rows, $columns, $cell(row, col) cell$text, cell$text_frame, cell$fill .data.frame(table) — extract cell text data frame

title: rpptx 0.1.0
text: Supported chart types: column, bar, line, pie, area, scatter (XY), bubble, radar, doughnut, variants CategoryChartData, XyChartData, BubbleChartData — data containers Chart proxy: $chart_title, $plots, $series, $legend

title: rpptx 0.1.0
